zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF於1495年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯時，他的父親Thomas Lowe I25歲，母親Mary FitzHugh24歲。他於1516年與Anne Reynes結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1563年9月12日在英國英格蘭倫敦享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lowe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Low  .

German (Löwe) and Danish (Løwe): from Middle High German lēwe, löuwe ‘lion’, hence a nickname for a brave or regal person. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. It is also found in Sweden. Compare Loewe 1, Lau , Lave , and Lurvey .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’.

variants.name: Lau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Wing, Kam, Kwok, Ming, Chi, Yuk, Chun, Kin, Ching, Kwan, Man.

German: nickname for a physically strong person, from Middle High German louwe, lauwe ‘lion’. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. Compare Lowe .
German: topographic name for someone living in a bush-covered area or clearing, Middle Low German lo, loch, lage, Middle High German lō.

variants.name: Loew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eckhard, Fritz, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Manfred, Raimund.
German (mainly Löw): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). The surname Loew is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Low 5.

variants.name: Lobe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Erwin, Horst, Otto, Winfried.
German (Löbe): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
German: metonymic occupational name for a tanner, from Middle High German lō ‘tanning bark’.

variants.name: Loewe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Armin, Gerhard, Helmuth, Klaus, Kurt.
German (mainly Löwe): see Lowe 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’. Compare Lowe 3.

variants.name: Loewen
meanings: German (Löwen): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). Compare Lowen 2.

variants.name: Low
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived near a tumulus, mound or hill, Middle English lowe, from Old English hlāw (see Law 2).

Scottish and English: nickname for a short man, from Middle English lah, lowe (Old Norse lágr; the word was adopted first into the northern dialects of Middle English, where Scandinavian influence was strong, and then spread south, with regular alteration of the vowel quality).

English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a violent or dangerous person, from Anglo-Norman French lou, leu ‘wolf’ (from Latin lupus). Wolves were relatively common in Britain at the time when most surnames were formed, as there still existed large tracts of uncleared forest.

variants.name: Lurvey
meanings: Americanized form of German Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
History: Peter Lurvy, the immigrant ancestor of this family, was in Ipswich, MA, by 1679.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Esq
meanings: Cornish: A Cornish form of Just (Prouter Est, the priest of St. Just).

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: St.
meanings: Cornish: A Cornish form of Just (Prouter Est, the priest of St. Just).

nameMeanings.name: Albans
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin name Albanus, which is of uncertain origin. It may be an ethnic name from one of the numerous places in the Roman Empire called Alba ‘white’, or it may represent a Latinized form of a British name derived from the Celtic word alp ‘rock, crag’. Christian tradition has it that St Alban was the first martyr in Roman Britain, who was executed, probably in 209 , at Verulamium (now known as St Albans). A Benedictine abbey was founded there and dedicated to the saint by King Offa . Derivation from Albion, a poetic name for Britain, is also a possibility.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF was born in 1495, in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Lowe I, was 25 and his mother, Mary FitzHugh, was 24. He married Anne Reynes in 1516. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 12 September 1563, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF est né en 1495, à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Lowe I, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mary FitzHugh, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Anne Reynes en 1516. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 12 septembre 1563, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGFが1495年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスで生まれた時，父Thomas Lowe Iは25歳，母Mary FitzHughは24歳であった。1516年にAnne Reynesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1563年9月12日にイギリスイギリスロンドンで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Esq Lord Of St. Albans Lowe 13th GGF(이)가 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Lowe I(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary FitzHugh(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1516년 Anne Reynes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1563년 9월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
